hard.next_step: 6.16.26 KK - no update - have gone quiet 5.26.26 KK - fu on workflows and next steps 5.12.26 KK - fu to julian and lead from gartner 4.22.26KK - good call with Julian bikash and devika. SLB to share requirements on what they are looking for
hard.ais_why: Good ANA discovery call with Julian in April 2026; SLB committed to sharing requirements. Opportunity is named SLB Merlin ANA 2026 and the customer asked for a prerecorded ANA demo showing the supplier-facing UI, an active buyer pull on the agentic motion.
hard.competitor: SAP Ariba (incumbent, being kept); Status quo / consulting-partner-gated selection; Others (unnamed autonomous-sourcing vendors)

north_star_verdict.headline: A $250k autonomous-negotiation add-on to SLB's Ariba stack that ran hot in April then went cold: the buyer has been silent 75+ days, no champion has formed, the CFO economic buyer has never engaged, and SLB parked the tool choice behind picking a consulting partner first.
north_star_verdict.math: 261 days to close (2027-03-24). Created 24 Mar 2026, 104 days old, ~81 days in Qualified with no progression to Formal Evaluation. Last genuine two-way buyer touch was the 22 Apr 2026 recap-email thread (75+ days ago); the 16 Apr discovery call was the last live buyer conversation. Everything since (Outreach Horizon-US emails/calls to Abdul Muqeet Mir and Laurent Geranton on 1-22 Jun) is rep-side outbound with no buyer reply. Next_Step 16 Jun reads 'no update - have gone quiet'. Cold 60+ days with no buyer-facing deliverable executed forces Off Track regardless of the far 2027 close date. To make the date credible SLB must re-engage, move to a scoped POC on the price-list use case within 30 days, and reach Shortlisted by Q4 2026; current pace (silent buyer, no champion, decision gated on a consulting-partner pick) does not support that.
evidence: Stage Qualified since 16 Apr 2026 with no progression; buyer silent since the 22 Apr 2026 recap thread (Next_Step 16 Jun 2026 'no update - have gone quiet').
Last live buyer conversation 16 Apr 2026 discovery call with Julian Howley (SF Meeting/Avoma 'External - SLB Sync and Discussion with Zycus').
Only activity in the last 60 days is rep-side Outreach (Horizon US invites to Abdul Muqeet Mir and Laurent Geranton, 1-22 Jun 2026) with no buyer response.
Decision is gated: Veronica Gouvea on 20 Mar 2026 said 'we're selecting a consulting partner. The choice of tool will come later. So for now it would be just discovery exploratory'.
No economic-buyer engagement: Stephane Biguet (EVP & CFO) is the Executive-Sponsor contact role but appears in no call, task or Next_Step.

No quantified ROI: pain is clear (autonomous negotiation of expiring price lists, sourcing velocity across four manufacturing subcategories) but no savings, cycle-time or cost figure has been captured against the $1B spend basis Veronica gave for pricing.

metrics.narrative: The operational pain is well articulated but never quantified. On 20 Mar 2026 Veronica Gouvea framed the core problem as multi-year contracts whose price lists expire quarterly or every six months and must be renegotiated without opening a full competitive bid ('the trigger would be expiration is coming up... we'd want the agent to contact the supplier... and come up to an agreement on the price list and push that back into Ariba or SAP'). On 16 Apr 2026 Julian Howley expanded it to strategic manufacturing sourcing velocity across four SLB-made subcategories (forging, machining parts, casting, fasteners) driven by a homegrown regression/target-price model, with the goal 'sourcing velocity and increasing spend on their sourcing'. No dollar savings, cycle-time reduction, headcount freed or error-rate target was ever stated; the only number on the table is the $1B spend volume Veronica gave purely as a pricing basis ('Can you just assume 1,000,000,000 and let us know what it would be'). Quantifying ROI against that $1B is the move that would give CFO Stephane Biguet a reason to fund $250k.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is mapped on paper but has zero engagement. Stephane Biguet (Executive Vice President & Chief Financial Officer) holds the Executive Sponsor contact role, and Laurent Geranton (Global Procurement Director) and Michael Scott / Nelma Tavares (Directors of Procurement) hold Decision Maker roles, so the power structure is named in Salesforce. None of them has appeared on a call, task or Next_Step note. The commercial conversation to date has been with evaluators only, and Karson has not moved to pricing precisely because internal buy-in is missing: Next_Step 1 Apr 2026 reads 'Not moving forwrd to share pricing as we need more buy in from SLB side as we have had many conversations with their team that doesnt go anywhere'. Budget authority is further gated behind SLB choosing a consulting partner before a tool, so no funding decision is live. The move is to earn an introduction from Veronica or Julian to Laurent Geranton and, ultimately, a CFO-level ROI conversation with Biguet.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Stephane Biguet, Laurent Geranton, Michael Scott, Nelma Tavares)

decision_criteria.narrative: Real evaluation criteria surfaced on both calls even though no formal RFP exists yet. Julian Howley on 16 Apr 2026 pressed hard on configurable, non-hard-coded workflows and human-in-the-loop guardrails ('how would you put in place those guardrail... certain pieces need to be completely autonomous... award recommendation, you don't do autonomous, it has to be hybrid'), on end-to-end autonomy from RFP to award, and on end-user (not admin) control. Veronica Gouvea on 20 Mar 2026 set integration as a gating criterion: SLB stays on SAP Ariba and needs Zycus to pull the PR from Ariba, run the autonomous bid, and push the PO back ('Would you be able to integrate and pull the PR from our current environment... generate the PO back in within Ariba'), plus true one-on-one supplier negotiation versus 'game theory within the sourcing platform'. Criteria are confirmed but not yet weighted or documented in a scorecard, and the price-list expiration-trigger workflow is an open capability question Karson still owes an answer on.

decision_process.narrative: Reading the call sequence, SLB is running a two-track, early-stage exploration, not a formal evaluation. Track one is Veronica Gouvea's tail-spend / spot-quoting price-list use case (20 Mar 2026), where she was explicit that process order is consulting-partner first, tool second: 'where the conversation is now is we're selecting a consulting partner. The choice of tool will come later'. Track two is Julian Howley's strategic manufacturing autonomous-sourcing initiative (16 Apr 2026), which he said is 'already moving very fast', though he was only forwarded into the Zycus call rather than driving vendor selection ('I mean, because it was sent to me'). Julian named Juliana Cardona and Natalia Nunes as the people 'driving all of these' on the tail-spend side; both appear in SLB email activity (jcardona8@slb.com, nnunes@slb.com) but have never met Zycus live. The approval chain (multi-stakeholder procurement leadership under a CFO sponsor) is mapped by contact role but untested. Net: no compelling event or dated decision milestone is set, and the process is stalled pending SLB's own consulting-partner choice.
sources: SF email activity 2-6 Apr 2026 (Juliana Cardona, Natalia Nunes)

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have been touched, which is appropriate this early but leaves the paper path invisible. The only commercial signal is a scope and licensing preference: on both calls SLB signalled a per-module purchase rather than the full suite (Julian 16 Apr 'Most likely, for us, we'd just be interested in the sourcing piece'; Veronica 20 Mar confirmed 'you're not buying the full source to pay suite. You're buying the sourcing and maybe the autonomous negotiation component'), and pricing is spend-based on ~$1B with a separate one-time implementation range. SLB also intends a third-party consulting partner to run initial implementation while SLB sustains it, which adds a partner into any future SOW. Nothing on MSA, Order Form, legal, procurement channel or InfoSec exists yet. Visibility would come from getting the ballpark commercials in front of the buyer and confirming who owns procurement sign-off (Laurent Geranton).

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is concrete and buyer-stated. Veronica Gouvea owns the tail-spend pain: multi-year contracts with periodically expiring price lists that today require manual renegotiation, and a strategic tail-spend initiative to bring more spend under management via autonomous spot quoting (20 Mar 2026). Julian Howley owns the strategic-sourcing pain: automating RFP-to-award across four complex SLB-manufactured subcategories with a validated target-price model, while keeping human checkpoints for data quality and awards (16 Apr 2026 'the main goal for us is sourcing velocity and increasing spend on their sourcing'). Both are confirmed pains articulated directly by the pain owners on calls. What is inferred, not confirmed, is the financial magnitude of that pain, which is why the business case remains partial.

champion.narrative: No champion has formed. Julian Howley (Process Improvement Leader, Global Procurement) is the most engaged buyer, ran a substantive 16 Apr 2026 discovery call and committed to send a written use-case recap 'with a little bit more nuances', but that recap never arrived and he has gone silent, so his engagement reads as interested-but-passive rather than advocacy. Veronica Gouvea (Supply Chain Performance Manager) opened the relationship on 20 Mar 2026 and requested the demo and commercials, but has shown no internal selling and has not re-engaged. Neither has introduced Zycus to the CFO sponsor, the procurement Decision Makers, or the named project drivers Juliana Cardona and Natalia Nunes. There is no one pushing Zycus internally or defending it against the status quo, and building a champion (most likely developing Julian, or reaching the real drivers) is the single highest-leverage recovery move.

competition.narrative: This is not a head-to-head against a named rival; the field is the status quo and an incumbent SLB intends to keep. Veronica Gouvea was explicit on 20 Mar 2026 that SLB runs SAP Ariba and does not want to replace it ('we're not looking to change away from Ariba, we wanna kinda build around it on top'), so Zycus's ANA / iSource must position as an autonomous-negotiation layer that integrates with Ariba (pull PR, push PO), not a suite rip-and-replace. The strongest current threat is inertia: SLB decided to pick a consulting partner before choosing a tool and then went quiet, so 'no decision / not now' is the live risk, not a competitor win. Competitors__c also lists 'Others' (unnamed autonomous-sourcing vendors) consistent with Veronica's 'we're future proofing... looking at many things', a diffuse exploratory field with no stated preference. We win by tying ANA to the expiring-price-list compelling event and proving Ariba integration in a scoped POC.

deal_movement.summary: Deal ran hot in late March and April then cooled to silence. Last genuine two-way buyer touch was the 22 Apr recap thread; the 16 Apr discovery call was the last live conversation. May and June are entirely rep-side outbound (Outreach Horizon-US invites) with no buyer reply, and Next_Step 16 Jun records the buyer 'gone quiet'.

items.change: Discovery call with Julian Howley (autonomous-sourcing use case walked through, demo agreed as next step)
Karson sent recap/next-steps email thread; buyer's last genuine reply window
Rep-side re-engagement attempts logged (fu to Julian and Gartner lead)
Rep-side Outreach Horizon-US invites to Laurent Geranton, Abdul Muqeet Mir, Simon Ross with no buyer reply
Next_Step updated to 'no update - have gone quiet'
Further Outreach follow-ups to Abdul Muqeet Mir and Laurent Geranton, no response

competitive_position.summary: The strongest CURRENT threat is the status quo / no-decision: on 20 Mar 2026 SLB decided to select a consulting partner before choosing any tool and has since gone quiet, so inertia, not a rival, is what loses this deal. SAP Ariba is the INCUMBENT SLB intends to keep and build around, so it is a stay-put pressure and an integration requirement rather than a displacement fight (corrects the prior read that framed Ariba as a rival Zycus is 'late against'). 'Others' (unnamed autonomous-sourcing vendors) is a diffuse exploratory field with no stated preference. No competitor is preferred or ahead; the buyer has expressed no lean toward any named rival.

competitors.how_we_win: Tie ANA to the expiring-price-list renegotiation cycle as a compelling event and convert exploratory interest into a scoped, dated POC before SLB's partner selection freezes the process.

competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on truly agentic, single-data-core autonomous negotiation versus rule-based 'game theory' automation the buyer flagged skepticism about.

best_practice_check.summary: Recovery playbook for a cold, early, un-championed deal in a marquee account: re-open the relationship, reach the real drivers, and anchor to a compelling event.
flags: Re-open the cold thread now: buyer silent 75+ days, so an owner-led re-engagement to Julian Howley and Veronica Gouvea delivering the overdue demo and commercials is the single highest-leverage move before the consulting-partner pick freezes the process.
Multi-thread to the actual drivers: Juliana Cardona and Natalia Nunes run the tail-spend track and have never met Zycus, so secure an introduction through Veronica.
Attach to a compelling event: tie ANA to the expiring-price-list renegotiation cycle and quantify savings on the $1B spend so CFO Stephane Biguet has a reason to fund $250k.

champion_strength.summary: No champion has formed. Julian Howley is the best candidate after the 16 Apr call but stayed passive, never sent the promised written recap, and has gone silent; Veronica Gouvea opened the door but shows no internal selling and neither has reached the CFO sponsor or the Decision Makers. Developing Julian or reaching the real drivers Juliana Cardona and Natalia Nunes is the top recovery priority.
champion_strength.source: Avoma call 16 Apr 2026 (Julian Howley); SF Next_Step 22 Apr and 16 Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The agentic story landed with the buyer: Julian engaged deeply on guardrails, autonomy levels and human-in-the-loop control, and the opportunity is named around Merlin/ANA. Positioning is strong; the risk is not positioning but a silent buyer and a gated decision.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 8.0 / Strong is corroborated by the calls: SLB explicitly sought an autonomous, agentic negotiation solution ('I found that you had an autonomous agentic solution, so that's why I reached out'), and Julian probed autonomy and guardrails in depth on 16 Apr. Genuine buyer pull on the agentic motion, not rep-inflated.

items.detail: CFO economic buyer Stephane Biguet and procurement Decision Makers (Geranton, Scott, Tavares) have never engaged; the deal has only touched evaluators.
items.source: SF Next_Step 1 Apr 2026; contact roles; rep Outreach 22 Jun 2026

items.detail: SLB decided to choose a consulting partner before selecting a tool, so the tool decision is parked and no dated milestone exists.
No quantified ROI captured against the $1B spend basis to justify $250k at CFO level.
Scope narrowed defensively from full suite to sourcing + ANA only, and SLB wants to keep Ariba and build around it, signalling low-priority, phased intent.
The expiration-triggered price-list renegotiation workflow into Ariba/SAP is an unconfirmed capability Zycus still owes an answer on.
items.source: Avoma call 20 Mar 2026 (Karson Keogh took it to product)

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cold and cooling: strong April interest has decayed to a 75+ day buyer silence with only unanswered rep-side outbound. Verdict trajectory is WEAKER versus the last sweep.
items: Buyer silent since the 22 Apr 2026 recap thread; Next_Step 16 Jun 'no update - have gone quiet'.
May-June activity is entirely rep-side Outreach with no buyer reply.
Decision gated behind SLB's consulting-partner selection with no dated milestone.

scope_change.from: Full source-to-contract / source-to-pay suite (iContract, iSource, Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA)
scope_change.to: Sourcing (iSource) plus the ANA autonomous negotiation agent only
scope_change.detail: Julian Howley on 16 Apr 2026 said 'Most likely, for us, we'd just be interested in the sourcing piece', and Veronica Gouvea on 20 Mar 2026 confirmed 'you're not buying the full source to pay suite. You're buying the sourcing and maybe the autonomous negotiation component'. Buyer is cutting to a phased, add-on scope around their retained Ariba stack, a defensive narrowing.

customer_preference.evidence: Neutral, exploratory posture: 'for now it would be just discovery exploratory' and 'we're future proofing... looking at many things' (Veronica, 20 Mar 2026); buyer then went silent. No Zycus-specific lean.

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (autonomous negotiation of expiring price lists, sourcing velocity across four manufacturing subcategories) but no quantified savings, cycle-time or cost figure; $1B is only a pricing basis, not an ROI.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A $250k autonomous-negotiation add-on to SLB's Ariba stack that ran hot in April then went cold: no champion has formed, the CFO economic buyer has never engaged, and SLB parked the tool choice behind picking a consulting partner first, so no-decision is the live risk.

win_position.text: We are behind on this deal: after a strong 16 Apr discovery call with Julian Howley the buyer has been silent 75+ days ('no update - have gone quiet', 16 Jun), no champion is advocating, and SLB's CFO economic buyer Stephane Biguet has never engaged.
The agentic fit is real and buyer-validated: SLB actively sought an autonomous negotiation agent and probed guardrails and autonomy in depth, with AIS at 8.0/Strong.

The decision is gated by SLB, not us: Veronica Gouvea said on 20 Mar they will select a consulting partner before choosing a tool, so there is no dated path to a decision and inertia is the biggest threat to the win.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has stalled: the last live buyer conversation was 16 Apr and every May-June touch is unanswered rep-side Outreach to Laurent Geranton and Abdul Muqeet Mir.
The deal has sat in Qualified since 16 Apr with no progression to Formal Evaluation despite April urgency for a fast POC.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is thin: SLB requested a prerecorded demo and ballpark commercials but framed everything as 'just discovery exploratory' and never named a decision date.
Julian Howley committed on 16 Apr to send a written use-case recap so Zycus could build the tailored demo and never delivered it, signalling low urgency.

deal_risk.text: No economic-buyer or Decision-Maker engagement: only evaluators have been reached, and Next_Step 1 Apr notes pricing is held back for lack of internal buy-in.
Scope has narrowed defensively from full suite to sourcing + ANA on a retained Ariba stack, and no ROI has been quantified to justify $250k to the CFO.

champion.evidence: No champion; best candidate Julian Howley passive and silent, promised recap never sent.

buyer_engagement.evidence: Buyer silent 75+ days; only unanswered rep-side outbound since 22 Apr.

economic_buyer.evidence: CFO Stephane Biguet and procurement DMs never engaged.
